passage: The university's writing center offers free tutoring to all enrolled students. Trained peer tutors help with everything from brainstorming ideas to polishing final drafts. The center's director, along with a team of graduate assistants, [1]_____ workshops on academic writing skills throughout the semester.

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from a university services guide

stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: This question tests subject-verb agreement with the phrase 'along with.' Unlike 'and,' the phrase 'along with' does not create a compound subject. The true subject remains 'The center's director' (singular). The parenthetical 'along with a team of graduate assistants' does not change the subject's number. Present tense 'offers' matches the ongoing, habitual activity.

distractorRationale.A: Base form/plural—disagrees with singular 'director.' Students might be misled by 'assistants' nearby.
distractorRationale.B: Correct. Singular present agreeing with 'The center's director.'
distractorRationale.C: Past tense—conflicts with present-tense description of ongoing services.
distractorRationale.D: Present perfect plural—disagrees with singular subject and implies a completed timeframe rather than an ongoing pattern.
